title: Council Tax Going Up in England
summary: Council tax bills in England will go up by 4.9% on average starting in April. This means people will pay more money for local services like libraries and bin collection. The average bill for a Band D home will be £2,392 a year, which is £111 more than last year. Councils are raising taxes because they need more money to pay for services, especially to help vulnerable people. Some places like Shropshire will see bigger increases, while others like Hartlepool will have smaller ones. The increase is higher than the inflation rate of 3%, making things more expensive for families. Council tax is a charge on properties that funds local services, and councils are allowed to raise it by up to 5% in many cases.

questions.question: Is the council tax increase higher or lower than inflation?

background_read: Council tax is a fee that people in England pay to their local council. It helps pay for things like taking out the trash, cleaning streets, and helping people who need care. Councils decide how much to charge based on the value of homes. Sometimes, they raise the tax to get more money when costs go up. This article talks about how much it will increase in April and why.
Article_Structure: WHO: Councils in England and people who pay council tax
WHAT: Council tax bills are going up by 4.9% on average
WHERE: In England, with examples like Shropshire and Hartlepool
WHY: To get more money for local services and help vulnerable people

perspectives.description: Councils need to raise taxes to pay for important services like social care.
